executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with at least 104 organizations victimized in a coordinated 48-hour attack wave. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate gangs), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware group alone claimed 20+ victims including the Eastern Townships School Board, while the Clop gang attacked 14 organizations. Critical infrastructure sectors hit hardest are healthcare, education, public services, and financial services—signaling a deliberate targeting of essential Canadian services.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a critical ransomware emergency with 104+ organizations compromised across all provinces in 48 hours. Public sector entities (municipalities, schools, hospitals, public health) are disproportionately affected, suggesting coordinated targeting of essential services. No CCCS advisories were identified in this dataset, though the scale of attacks warrants immediate federal coordination.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a confirmed database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers was hit by Sinobi ransomware. Multiple accounting firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant) were compromised.

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA and NCSC are warning that Cisco ASA and Firepower devices are under active exploitation via three N-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) enabling unauthorized access—Canada's CCCS has issued a matching advisory (AL25-012). The npm supply chain was hit hard this week: Bitwarden CLI 2026.4.0 was compromised and pushed malicious code that steals tokens, SSH keys, and cloud credentials, while a separate worm-like npm campaign (Shai-Hulud malware via TeamPCP) targets developers. UK Biobank suffered a massive breach with health data of 500,000 people now for sale on Alibaba in China. North Korea's Lazarus group is using AI-enhanced ClickFix social engineering to target macOS users, and ShinyHunters claims breaches of Udemy, Anthropic's Claude Mythos model data, and Carnival Corporation (7.5M accounts).

items.description: Cisco ASA and Firepower vulnerability enabling unauthorized access

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Vulnerability in GPL Odorizers or OpenClaw
RTF exploit used to install uWarrior RAT (Italian origin threat actor)
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.name: Lazarus Group (APT38, Hidden Cobra)
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1204 (User Execution via ClickFix social engineering), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), AI-enhanced phishing lures

items.name: TeamPCP
items.targets: Software developers using npm packages and Bitwarden CLI
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise - Software Supply Chain), T1543 (Create or Modify System Process via Dependabot), credential theft, SSH key exfiltration, worm-like propagation (Shai-Hulud malware)

items.name: UNC6692
items.targets: Corporate IT environments via Microsoft Teams
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing - Spearphishing Attachment), T1656 (Impersonation - IT Helpdesk), T1204 (User Execution), deploys SNOW malware

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Udemy (7.5M accounts), Anthropic Claude Mythos model, Carnival Corporation (7.5M accounts), ADT Inc.
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage Object), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), credential databases offered for sale on BreachForums and similar marketplaces

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), double extortion tactics

items.name: HexDex
items.targets: Multiple organizations (French police arrested 20-year-old operator preparing to leak additional stolen data)
items.ttps: T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel), data theft and public leak on BreachForums

items.name: Scattered Spider (0ktapus, UNC3944)
items.targets: Cryptocurrency sector, corporate VPN and SSO systems
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing - Spearphishing Link), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1110 (Brute Force), SIM swapping, social engineering, member pleaded guilty in US federal court for theft of $8M+ in cryptocurrency

items.name: Unknown Italian APT
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing - Spearphishing Attachment via weaponized RTF), T1203 (Exploitation for Client Execution - CVE-2012-1856, CVE-2015-1770), deploys uWarrior RAT

items.name: FIRESTARTER APT
items.targets: Cisco ASA and Firepower devices (public-facing infrastructure)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application - CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363), T1505.003 (Server Software Component - Web Shell), persistence on network perimeter devices

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 on April 23, 2026, warning that Cisco ASA and Firepower devices are under active exploitation via CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, and CVE-2025-20363. Canadian organizations using these devices must patch immediately. Broader Canadian retail and financial sectors showed no specific incidents this week, though supply chain attacks targeting npm and developer tools present indirect risk to Canadian software firms.
